title: Notifications page should support filtering and searching
description:
The Notifications tab on the Push Notifications page should allow filtering and searching of notifications by Title,
Body, Stats (All, Successful, Failed as booleans based >0 notifications of those types) and date ranges for Created
and Sent.

title: Test Document cleanup
description:
Move the JSON sections out of the main body of the created Test Documents into a separate Tab called JSON.  The first tab should be named "Results".

The structure of the JSON tab should be the same as the per-test results in the Results tab, but with just the Test Header (model, image, size, etc) and the JSON.
acceptance_criteria:
- created Test Document contains two Tabs: Results and JSON
- Tests pass

title: "Deliveries" should be paged in the same way that "Notifications" and "Subscribers" are.
Search Testing should allow user-entered intents
description:
The Upload Image and Image Library tabs in Search Testing should allow users to enter their own intents (in addition
to showing the intents generated by the Intent Analysis subsystem).

title: The Intent Analysis prompt should automatically include all Test Images as options on the Run Tests tab rather than only those linked to it.
Add a prompt_history table that tracks changes made to the prompts table (e.g. changing status, name, description, etc)
Missing image causes Prompt Testing to get stuck forever.
description:
When using the Prompt Testing feature, if an image is missing, the process hangs indefinitely instead of failing
gracefully. It should show an error for the missing image and continue processing other images, then complete the
test run (e.g. create the spreadsheet, update the UI, etc).

Here is an example document that shows two empty rows, corresponding to missing images, in the Summary table:

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yt0D6PKB3eDdjd8AQlBgCbekY_3JtC1tjARM5D5OMNI

I see these errors in the logs:
```
Jan 25 15:37:35 ip-172-31-71-152.ec2.internal uvicorn[153501]: ERROR:     lib.s3_storage - Failed to download image test-images/handwriting_johann_sebastian_bach.png: An error occurred (NoSuchKey) when calling the GetObject operation: The specified key does not exist.
```

title: Error when trying to change the Default Model for a system prompt
description: When I tried to change the Default Model for the Intent Analysis Prompt from OpenAI:GPT-5-nano to "OpenAI: GPT-4.1-mini" the system showed an "Object" error message after I clicked the Save button and did not change the value.

title: Remove the extra confirmation step when sending push notifications
Test button doesn't work
description:
When I first click on a Test button, it takes me to Prompt Testing but does not select the project. If I refresh the page, then it selects
the project.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clicking on the Test button should select that project on the Prompt Testing page.
- Tests pass

title: Subscribers page should support paging
description:
The Subscribers tab on the Push Notifications page currently loads all subscribers at once. It should show
one page at a time, using the same paging controls as other pages in admin UI (e.g. Request Viewer).

title: All of the documentation in the Admin UI (e.g. https://admin.photoop.com/docs.html) should require the user to be authenticated
Improve Search Testing UX
description:
When the user clicks on "Search Prompts", there should be an immediate visual indication that the system is
processing the request. It should also hide any stale results from previous searches to avoid confusion.

title: Notifications page should support paging
description:
The Notifications tab on the Push Notifications page currently loads all notifications at once. It should show
one page at a time, usuing the same paging controls as other pages in admin UI (e.g. Request Viewer).

title: Make the tabs in the various pages (e.g. Push Notifications) persistent via URL, so a refresh will retain the tab
Remove the document emoji from the "Google Doc" column on the Prompt Management page
The paging buttons on the Intent Analysis page should only be big enough to fit the images.
Analyze Search Fields
description:
The Prompt Management system runs a series of analyses on prompts when the "Check" button is clicked. Extend the current
search field analysis to sanity check the values in these fields.

The Photo Keywords field should be checked to ensure it contains only relevant keywords that could plausibly
describe the content of a photo.

The Intent Keywords field should be checked to ensure it contains only relevant keywords that could plausibly
describe an intent satisfied by the prompt.

The Intent Matches field should be checked to ensure the items in the list follow the pattern of intents generated by the Intent
Analysis subsystem. These are generally 3-5 word declarative phrases, such as "repair paint damage" or "appraise
jewelry value". They are never questions or long sentences. They should plausibly describe intents that the prompt
can satisfy.

title: Link "Try" button to Prompt Testing
description:
Currently the "Try" button next to each prompt in the Prompt Management page takes the user to the App Simulation page, with the Choose Project prepopulated with the prompt.

Change this button to "Test" and have it go to the Prompt Testing page, still with the project selected.

title: Subscribers page should support filtering and searching
description:
The Subscribers tab on the Push Notifications page should allow filtering and searching of subscribers
by User, App, and Status, as well as date ranges for Created and Last Used.

title: Make search criteria (Intent Domains, Capability Tags, and Sample Phrases) read-only in Prompt Management UI.
Make the search field importer verify that all three section markers are found in the SEARCH FIELD section. If not, show a warning (similar to the warnings about missing markers).
The Prompt Match Investigator should show all of the data returned by the API, even if none of the prompts meet the threshold.
Change the TESTING section label in the navigation pane to DEVELOPMENT & TESTING
Fix intent_analyses table not saving model field
description:
The model field in the intent_analyses table is not being populated for recent requests. Since Jan 27, 2026, all 81+ intent analysis requests have NULL in the model column, while the request_logs table correctly records the model used.

## Investigation

- intent_analyses: 81 requests with NULL model since Jan 27
- request_logs: correctly shows gpt-5-mini, gpt-4o, etc. for the same period

## Expected Behavior

The model should be saved when creating/updating intent_analyses records so we can track performance metrics by model directly from that table.

## Files to Investigate

- app/routes/intent.py (two-phase API endpoints)
- Look for where intent_analyses records are created/updated

title: Add the ability to Select All or None of the images on the Prompt Testing page. Default to only selecting the first image in the list.
"Roll up" requests in Request Viewer
description:
When viewing a list of Requests in the Request Viewer, they should be "rolled up" into groups the same way that Related Requests are grouped, with one entry for the group.

The group line should show the image thumbnail, number of members, timestamps of the first and last members of the group,
the first few words of the Image Description, if it is available, environment, and totals for tokens, prompt, and completion

The user should be able to expand a group in order to see them "unrolled", although they should still be shown in a group (rather than interleaved with requests from other groups, like they are now).

The user should also be able to select an "Ungrouped" view, which is what it looks like now.

title: Consolidate /db/migrations and /db/migrations_yoyo into the latter and remove the old /db/migrations to avoid confusion
test_product_validation tests are failing due to missing await on async method
Add Delete button to Push Notifications page
description:
The Notifications tab on the Push Notifications page shows all of the notifications but the only action it allows
is "view". There should be a "Delete" button next to each notification to allow users to remove notifications they
no longer need.
design:
The Delete button should look similar to the View button and appear to the right of it. When clicked, a confirmation
dialog should appear asking the user to confirm the deletion. Upon confirmation, the notification should be removed
from the list and a success message should be displayed.

title: Augment the documentation in docs/FEEDBACK_API.md with the example implementation found on the results page of the App Simulation page
Improve Search Debugging
description:
The Prompt Search page should include debugging information to help developers understand how search queries are
processed and why certain results are returned. This could include details such as tokenization, matching algorithms,
and relevance scoring.

For example, the expandable section for each search result should be extended to also show the score breakdown
and calculation of the final score.

title: Create Template Prompt Document
description:
Currently, the Create New Prompt form requires "Google Doc ID or URL" to be specified. 
Add a button next to that field which will create a Google Doc, owned by the current user and shared with the service account.
Once the document has been created, put its Google Doc ID into the form field.

The document should be titled using the Display Name, if that field has already been filled out in the form.

If the form already has a Description field, that should be included at the top (before the "=== START PROMPT ===" marker).

The document should contain the standard section markings ("== START PROMPT ===", "=== END PROMPT ===", etc).

The "SEARCH FIELDS" section of the document should be prepopulated with the required headers.

See https://admin.photoop.com/docs.html#PROMPT_SEARCH for details on search fields.

title: The Image Sizes checkbox in Prompt Testing for the Intent Analysis prompt should default to the value stored with that prompt
Automated generate-image-analysis.service can commit unrelated uncommitted changes
description:
The generate-image-analysis.timer runs daily at 10:00 UTC and commits plot files. However, if there are uncommitted changes in the working tree from ongoing work, the script can interfere with the git state.

Root cause: The service uses 'git commit --only' which should only commit the listed files, but timing issues with staged files can cause unrelated changes to get bundled.

Required behavior: The script should generate and commit ONLY the plot files without affecting any other uncommitted changes in the repository.

Suggested fix: Use git stash to preserve working changes during the operation:

git stash push --keep-index --quiet || true
# generate plots and commit
git stash pop --quiet || true

Or use a separate git worktree for the automated commits.

title: Add System Settings page to manage prompt search weights and threshold
description:
Create a new 'System Settings' page in the MANAGEMENT section of the sidebar that allows admins to:
- View and edit prompt search weights (photo_keywords_weight, intent_keywords_weight, intent_matches_weight)
- View and edit the min_score_threshold
- Save changes to the database settings table

The settings are currently stored in the app_settings table and read via SettingsService.get_prompt_search_weights(). Need to add:
1. PUT/POST endpoint to update settings
2. New static/system_settings.html page
3. Sidebar link in MANAGEMENT section
4. Update PROMPT_SEARCH.md documentation to reference the new UI

Current defaults:
- photo_keywords_weight: 1
- intent_keywords_weight: 2  
- intent_matches_weight: 10
- min_score_threshold: 0.25

title: Update the Prompt Search & Retrieval document to match the updated behavior.
description: For example, the only way to manage search metadata is in the Google Doc - the UI is read-only. Also, the example Intent Matches are short declarative phrases, not question sentences.

title: Create and store thumbnail images of the Image Library to speed up the display of pages that display lists of those image, like the Intent Analysis page.
Add keyword and tag filtering to the "Run Tests" tab in Prompt Testing to improve Test Image selection
Add the ability for an admin to temporarily switch to see what the UI looks like for a non-admin
Change "Default Model" to just "Model" when describing prompts
description:
The Add and Edit modals in the Prompt Management section show "Default Model" which is deceptive, as that is the model which will be used, not just some default.

There may be references to the model for System Prompts as well - these should also be changed.

Locations which say "Default Model" and that is in fact what the field means (that is, it might be overridden elsewhere) should be left alone.

Underlying data structures and database schemas should also be changed if they are misleading - e.g. "default_model" should be changed to "model" if the field is not a default, but rather a final value.
acceptance_criteria:
- All user-viewable representations of the model to be definitively used with a specific prompt should be "Model" rather than "Default Model"
- All user-viewable representations of default models should be left as "Default Model"
- Tests pass

title: Add the ability to copy an image from the Request Viewer to the Test Image Library

When viewing a Request in the Request Viewer, the user should be able to copy that image to the Test Image Library.

If the request is associated with a prompt, it should be automatically linked to that project.

Once the image has been added, it should be selected on the "Link Images to Projects" / "Select Image -> Add Projects" / "Select Image" page
so the user can select more projects for it. 

Once it has been linked, the user should be taken to the Manage Images page but only show that image in the list.

title: Fix Usage Statistics page
description: The "User Distribution by Days of Usage" and "User Distribution by Total Uses" graphs aren't showing Images Uploaded (they are in the legend, but not on the charts).
acceptance_criteria:

title: Disassociate Products from Prompts
description:
Products are currently tied to the prompt(s) used to create them. However, different prompts may suggest the same
product. To avoid duplication and improve efficiency, products should be decoupled from prompts.

It is still useful to know the source of a product, so we should maintain a relationship between products and
prompts but allow them to be many-to-many (or many-to-none - we will include products that were not created via
prompts at all).

When displaying products, don't show the ID or list of Prompts used to create them. Instead, show the prompt(s)
only when viewing the details of a specific product.

When adding a product, allow users to associate it with multiple prompts or none at all.

When checking for duplicate products, consider only the product attributes and ignore any information
about the prompts used to create them.

When deleting a prompt, do not delete the associated products. Instead, just remove the association
between the prompt and the products.

When deleting a product, ensure that any associations with prompts are also removed.

When loading products from a Google Doc, first check to see if the product is already in the database - don't
create duplicates. Add the association to the prompt if it doesn't already exist. Also, remove any associations
that are no longer valid (but do not delete the products themselves).

When checking to see if a product is curated, ignore any information about the prompts used to create them.

title: Implement getUserMedia camera to skip Android confirmation step
description:
Replace the HTML file input camera capture with getUserMedia API to provide direct camera stream access. This bypasses the native Android camera app entirely, eliminating the confirmation step (checkmark button) that users currently have to tap after taking a photo.

## Current Behavior
1. User taps camera button
2. Android camera app opens
3. User takes photo
4. User must tap checkmark to confirm (extra step)
5. Photo sent to app

## Proposed Behavior
1. User taps camera button
2. Camera stream appears in-app
3. User taps capture button
4. Photo immediately captured (no confirmation needed)

## Trade-offs
- Loses native camera features (zoom, flash controls, HDR, filters)
- More JavaScript code to maintain
- Need to handle camera permissions explicitly
- May need fallback to file input for unsupported browsers

## Implementation Notes
- Use navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia()
- Display video element with camera stream
- Capture frame to canvas on button tap
- Consider adding basic flash/torch toggle if supported

title: Install bd (beads) on dev server
Complete Transition to New PhotoOp Infrastructure
description:
There are three different repositories that need to be updated to fully transition to the new PhotoOp infrastructure:
photoop-infrastructure, image2project, and picture_this_demo_app

The photoop-infrastructure repository has already been updated. The image2project and picture_this_demo_app
repositories have support for both the old and new infrastructure (e.g. deploy.sh vs deploy-to-infrastructure.sh). The
final step is to update these repositories to use only the new infrastructure.

Update/consolidate deployment scripts to use the new infrastructure.

Ensure that deployments are working correctly to the new infrastructure, including setting all of the correct secrets
and environment variables. There are currently specific concerns around the VAPID keys used for push notifications.

Make sure that deployments to the new infrastructure are automated and documented.

Make sure that a new production server can be deployed and tested completely before being added to the target
group. This should include adding it to a staging "environment" (sharing the database and LB but using a different
target group) for testing before going live. The staging environment should be able to be torn down after testing. The
staging environment should be accessible externally using staging.photoop.com. This process should be automated as much
as possible, with manual steps and checkpoints as needed, and completely documented.

admin.photoop.com and dev.photoop.com should be routed to a target group containing just the dev server.

photoop.com and www.photoop.com should be routed to a target group containing the production servers.

Any references to jjkent.com, lovetoknow.com, or fink.com need to be called out so they can be updated or removed. Any
reference to a domain other than photoop.com (or its subdomains) should be reviewed.

The product and company is "PhotoOp".  Any references to "JJ Kent", "JJKent", or "LoveToKnow" should be reviewed and
updated as necessary.

All documentation needs to be updated to reflect the above.

comments.author: kevinfink

- dev.photoop.com → dev_app target group → dev EC2 ✓
- photoop.com/www → app target group → prod EC2 ✓
- api.photoop.com → api target group → prod EC2 ✓
- terraform validate succeeds ✓

Remaining work in OTHER repositories (image2project, picture_this_demo_app):

- Update legacy domain references (jjkent.com → photoop.com)

These should be tracked as tickets in those respective repositories.

comments.text:
Decomposed into sub-tasks across repositories:

**photoop-infrastructure:**
- photoop-infrastructure-y8e: Remove legacy jjkent SSM parameters and EC2 user data references
